article_meta.title: The Corner NR PLUS Trade Breaking News: Americans Pay the Tariffs Their Government Imposes on Them By John R

reasoning: The model identifies genuine bias instances in the author's own prose and correctly avoids flagging quoted text. The article is clearly an opinion/commentary piece with substantial author-level bias. The three items flagged are all defensible instances from author prose. However, the model misses several additional clear bias instances, and one bias type label is slightly debatable.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All three flagged items are from the author's own prose. The WSJ quoted passages and attributed research findings are correctly excluded. No attribution rule violations.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: All three flagged items are defensible. 'Farce' is genuinely opinionated author prose. The Greenland/punishment framing is the author's own characterization. 'Coercively redirects' and 'may be even worse' are subjective author language. The Mudslinging label for the Greenland item is slightly overreaching — it's more Spin or Sensationalism than Ad Hominem — but the text identification is correct.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model missed several instances: 'Can you identify the logical reasoning there?' (rhetorical sarcasm/Spin), 'Hold on a minute' (sarcastic author framing), 'Apparently not' (dismissive author commentary), 'Turns out Adam Smith got it right' (opinionated framing), the overall Slant of the article presenting only one side of the tariff debate. The instruction to be cautious with fewer confident examples partially justifies this, but key instances were still missed.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: The first item (Opinion Statements Presented as Fact) is correctly labeled. The third item (Subjective Qualifying Adjectives) is correctly labeled. The second item labeled as Mudslinging/Ad Hominem is debatable — the Greenland reference is factual (Trump did pursue Greenland acquisition), and the framing is more Spin/Sensationalism than personal attack. This is a minor mislabel.
